name: Justice N.V. Ramana
bio: Justice Nuthalapati Venkata Ramana served as the 48th Chief Justice of India from April 2021 to August 2022. During his tenure, he focused on addressing the backlog of cases, filling judicial vacancies, and strengthening the collegium system. He authored important judgments on the right to be forgotten, free speech, and the independence of the judiciary. Justice Ramana began his legal career as an advocate practising in the Andhra Pradesh High Court before being elevated to the bench.
